commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a ClawPack repair action/mutation, expands package scan backfill selection, adds mocked unit coverage, and updates the security moderation spec for artifact-backed package review.

Reproducibility: yes. Live package API checks still show npm-pack artifacts with npmFileCount greater than release.files, and current source routes package file/API/LLM surfaces through release.files.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should decide the production migration contract and require real Convex proof before merge; this security/moderation data repair is not a safe automated patch lane.

target.title: fix: repair historical ClawPack release files
target.body:
Fixes #1991

## Summary
- repair historical npm-pack ClawPack releases whose stored release file rows are shorter than the recorded artifact file count
- parse the stored artifact, persist implementation entries, recompute the release fingerprint, reset stale package review state, and queue static/Codex package scans after repair
- update the security moderation spec so ClawPack inspect/review surfaces use artifact implementation files instead of metadata-only rows

## Validation
- `bun run test convex/packages.public.test.ts --testNamePattern "ClawPack|backfill batch|backfill" --reporter=dot`
- `bun run format:check -- convex/packages.ts convex/packages.public.test.ts specs/security-moderation.md`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`
- `bun run ci:static`
- `bun run ci:unit`
- `bun run ci:types-build`

## Notes
- `bunx convex codegen` could not run in this fresh checkout because no `CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT` is configured; `bun run setup:worktree -- --quiet` also could not find a source worktree with `.env.local` and matching Convex local config.
- Local autoreview was attempted, but its nested runner got stuck after completing its own checks; the actionable integrity/package-state issue it surfaced has been fixed and the repo-native gates above were rerun.
target.author: turbotheturtle
